title: A Rare Miracle: Man Rescued After Eight Days Under Venezuela Earthquake Rubble
subtitle: International teams work together to find survivors as the death toll from the twin earthquakes continues to rise.

summary.en: A 43-year-old security guard was pulled alive from a collapsed building in Venezuela eight days after twin earthquakes. The rescue involved international teams from several countries working around the clock. While this success has brought hope to the nation, the official death toll has now passed 2,200 people. Many families are still waiting for news about their missing loved ones trapped under the wreckage. Rescue efforts remain difficult due to unstable buildings and dangerous weather conditions.
summary.zh: 一名四十三歲的保全在兩起地震發生八天後，從委內瑞拉一棟倒塌的建築中獲救。此次救援行動涉及多國團隊，他們不眠不休地進行搜救。儘管這次成功為國家帶來了希望，但官方死亡人數已超過二千二百人。許多家庭仍在等待失蹤親人的消息，他們可能仍受困於廢墟之下。由於建築結構不穩與危險的天氣狀況，救援工作依然困難重重。

paragraphs.en: In a moment of rare hope for a nation struggling with disaster, rescue workers pulled a 43-year-old man alive from the ruins of a collapsed building on Thursday. Hernán Alberto Gil Flores, a nightshift security guard, had been trapped for eight days following two powerful earthquakes that struck Venezuela. The 7.2- and 7.5-magnitude quakes hit the coastal region last week, causing widespread destruction and leaving thousands of people in need of help.
paragraphs.zh: 在一個飽受災難折磨的國家，出現了難得的希望時刻。週四，救援人員從一棟倒塌建築的廢墟中救出了一名四十三歲的男子。這名男子名叫赫南·阿爾貝托·吉爾·弗洛雷斯（Hernán Alberto Gil Flores），是一名夜班保全，在委內瑞拉發生兩起強烈地震後，他已被困長達八天。上週，該沿海地區遭遇規模七點二與七點五的地震襲擊，造成廣泛破壞，數千人亟需援助。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian, Gil Flores survived because his small security cabin stayed mostly intact, creating a vital pocket of air beneath the fallen concrete. A specialized team from the Costa Rican Red Cross first found signs of life on Sunday. Rescuers from several nations, including Chile, the United States, Portugal, and Mexico, worked together in a difficult operation to reach him. They used a telescopic camera to stay in contact with him and passed water and liquid nutrients through a narrow shaft to keep him hydrated during the final days of the rescue.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，吉爾·弗洛雷斯之所以能倖存，是因為他所在的小型保全崗亭大致保持完好，在倒塌的混凝土下形成了一個關鍵的空氣囊。來自哥斯大黎加紅十字會（Costa Rican Red Cross）的專業團隊於週日首次發現生命跡象。來自智利、美國、葡萄牙與墨西哥等多國的救援人員通力合作，進行了一場艱難的營救行動。他們使用伸縮式攝影機與他保持聯繫，並透過狹窄的通道傳送水與液態營養品，確保他在救援最後幾天內維持水分。

paragraphs.en: Cristian Vera, the leader of the Chilean rescue team, told Deutsche Welle (DW) that the building was a very complicated structure to enter. The teams had to work around the clock for three days to reach the exact spot where Gil Flores was trapped. During the final hours of the operation, a Chilean firefighter named María Paz Campos spoke to him constantly to keep him calm. When he was finally brought out on a stretcher, the scene was filled with cheers from international teams and local onlookers.
paragraphs.zh: 智利救援隊隊長克里斯蒂安·維拉（Cristian Vera）向德國之聲（DW）表示，該建築結構極其複雜，難以進入。救援隊必須連續三天不眠不休地工作，才能抵達吉爾·弗洛雷斯受困的確切位置。在行動的最後幾個小時，一名名叫瑪麗亞·帕茲·坎波斯（María Paz Campos）的智利消防員不斷與他對話，以穩定他的情緒。當他最終被抬上擔架救出時，現場充滿了國際團隊與當地圍觀民眾的歡呼聲。

paragraphs.en: Gusbimar González, the wife of Gil Flores, expressed her relief after fearing the worst. She told Deutsche Welle (DW) that she was surprised to see so many countries working together for a single cause. "I saw a ray of light in the darkness," she said, describing the rescue as a miracle. The acting Venezuelan President, Delcy Rodriguez, also shared her support on social media, thanking the international teams for their bravery and unity.
paragraphs.zh: 吉爾·弗洛雷斯的妻子古斯比瑪·岡薩雷斯（Gusbimar González）在經歷了最壞的擔憂後，終於鬆了一口氣。她告訴德國之聲，對於看到這麼多國家為了同一個目標攜手合作感到驚訝。她形容這次救援是一場奇蹟，並表示：「我在黑暗中看見了一道光。」委內瑞拉代理總統德爾西·羅德里奎茲（Delcy Rodriguez）也在社群媒體上表達支持，感謝國際團隊的英勇與團結。

paragraphs.en: Despite this success, the overall situation in Venezuela remains very serious. As reported by Deutsche Welle (DW), the official death toll has now passed 2,295 people. More than 11,000 others have been injured, and tens of thousands of people remain missing. NASA data suggests that nearly 60,000 buildings were damaged or destroyed by the twin quakes, leaving thousands of people homeless. While rescue teams continue to search, the chances of finding more survivors are decreasing quickly as time passes.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管取得了這次成功，委內瑞拉的整體局勢依然非常嚴峻。據德國之聲報導，官方統計的死亡人數現已超過二千二百九十五人。另有一萬一千多人受傷，數萬人下落不明。美國國家航空暨太空總署（NASA）的數據顯示，這兩起地震導致近六萬棟建築受損或毀壞，造成數千人無家可歸。隨著時間流逝，救援隊雖然持續搜尋，但找到更多倖存者的機率正迅速下降。

paragraphs.en: Many families are still waiting for news at the sites of collapsed buildings. Dora Bello, whose nephew is still missing, told The Guardian that they need more action to find those who might still be alive. "There is life inside that building," she said while standing near the wreckage of a tower block. The rescue teams are facing many challenges, including unstable structures, heavy rain, and constant aftershocks — smaller earthquakes that happen after a major one. These conditions make the work slow and dangerous for everyone involved.
paragraphs.zh: 許多家庭仍在倒塌建築的現場等待消息。多拉·貝洛（Dora Bello）的姪子至今仍失蹤，她告訴《衛報》，他們需要採取更多行動來尋找可能還活著的人。她站在一棟倒塌大樓的殘骸旁說：「那棟建築裡還有生命。」救援隊正面臨許多挑戰，包括結構不穩、大雨以及持續不斷的餘震（即主震後發生的較小地震）。這些狀況使得救援工作對所有參與者而言都變得緩慢且危險。

paragraphs.en: The government of Venezuela is currently facing criticism from some citizens who believe the response to the disaster has been too slow. However, officials have defended the efforts, highlighting the complexity of the damage and the importance of international cooperation. As the cleanup process begins in many areas, the focus remains on finding anyone who might still be trapped. While the rescue of Gil Flores has provided a brief moment of joy, the country faces a long and difficult road to recovery in the weeks and months ahead.
paragraphs.zh: 委內瑞拉政府目前面臨部分民眾的批評，認為對災難的反應過於遲緩。然而，官員們為救援工作辯護，強調損害的複雜性以及國際合作的重要性。隨著許多地區開始清理工作，焦點仍放在尋找任何可能受困的人員。雖然吉爾·弗洛雷斯的獲救帶來了短暫的喜悅，但該國在未來的幾週與幾個月內，仍將面臨漫長且艱難的復原之路。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To hit or attack suddenly and with force.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 襲擊、衝擊（指災害突然發生）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「打擊」或「罷工」，這裡指地震突然發生。文中：The 7.2- and 7.5-magnitude quakes hit the coastal region last week, causing widespread destruction and leaving thousands of people in need of help.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Happening or working continuously, 24 hours a day.
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面上看起來與時鐘有關，但實際上是指不間斷地工作。文中：The teams had to work around the clock for three days to reach the exact spot where Gil Flores was trapped.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The most serious or unpleasant event that could happen.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作形容詞（最差的），這裡作名詞使用，指代死亡或最糟的結果。文中：She told Deutsche Welle (DW) that she was surprised to see so many countries working together for a single cause.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A process or journey towards a specific future outcome.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作實體道路，這裡比喻國家復原的過程。文中：While the rescue of Gil Flores has provided a brief moment of joy, the country faces a long and difficult road to recovery in the weeks and months ahead.

sources.title: Venezuelan man saved from collapsed mall eight days after earthquakes

sources.title: Venezuelan man rescued alive 8 days after powerful quakes

previousArticles.title: A Rare Moment of Hope Amidst Venezuela’s Earthquake Crisis
previousArticles.summaryEn: A two-year-old boy was rescued from collapsed buildings in Venezuela six days after twin earthquakes struck the region. The official death toll has climbed past 2,200 as rescue operations continue across the country. Tens of thousands of people remain without shelter, food, or clean water. International aid is arriving, but local residents report that relief efforts are moving too slowly. The United Nations warns that millions of people may be affected by this disaster.

previousArticles.title: Rescue Efforts Continue in Venezuela as Death Toll Climbs
previousArticles.summaryEn: Venezuela is struggling to recover after two powerful earthquakes struck the country last week. The official death toll has now risen to over 1,700 people, with thousands more injured or missing. International rescue teams are working alongside local citizens to search through the ruins of collapsed buildings. While some survivors have been found, experts warn that the window for finding people alive is closing. The United States and other nations have sent significant aid to help with the recovery process.

previousArticles.title: Rescue Efforts Continue in Venezuela as Death Toll Rises After Twin Earthquakes
previousArticles.summaryEn: Venezuela is struggling to recover after two powerful earthquakes struck the country last week. The official death toll has now climbed to over 1,700 people, with thousands more injured or missing. International rescue teams are working alongside local citizens to find survivors in the rubble. The United Nations is preparing for a high number of casualties as the critical window for rescue closes. Meanwhile, the United States has increased its financial aid to support the massive relief operation.

quiz.question: How were rescue workers able to keep Hernán Alberto Gil Flores alive during the final days of the operation?

options.A: They used a specialized crane to lift the concrete slabs off his cabin.
options.B: They provided him with water and liquid nutrients through a narrow shaft.
options.C: They entered the cabin directly to provide him with food and medical care.
options.D: They used a telescopic camera to monitor his health and deliver medicine.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，救援人員透過狹窄的通道（narrow shaft）運送水和液體營養品，以維持他的水分與體力。選項 A 錯誤，文中未提到使用起重機移除混凝土。選項 C 錯誤，救援人員直到最後才進入現場，並非在最後幾天直接進入。選項 D 錯誤，伸縮攝影機是用來與他保持聯繫，而非運送藥物。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the rescue of Hernán Alberto Gil Flores?

options.A: It was a routine operation that the Chilean team had practiced many times.
options.B: It was the only successful rescue mission conducted by the international teams.
options.C: It required a high level of coordination between multiple international organizations.
options.D: It was completed quickly because the building structure was not severely damaged.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第二段與第三段，救援行動涉及智利、美國、葡萄牙、墨西哥及哥斯大黎加紅十字會等多國團隊，且救援過程極為困難，需要高度的跨國合作。選項 A 錯誤，文中提到這是一個困難的行動（difficult operation），並非例行公事。選項 B 錯誤，文中未說這是唯一成功的救援。選項 D 錯誤，第三段明確指出該建築結構非常複雜，救援相當困難。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'road' mean in the phrase 'the country faces a long and difficult road to recovery'?

options.A: A physical path or highway that needs to be rebuilt.
options.B: A metaphorical process or journey toward a future state.
options.C: A specific plan created by the government for construction.
options.D: A period of time during which rescue operations will continue.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此語境中，road 是隱喻用法，指國家從災難中復原的「過程」或「歷程」，而非實體的道路。選項 A 錯誤，這是字面意思的誤解。選項 C 錯誤，文中並未提到具體的政府建設計畫。選項 D 錯誤，road 在此處指涉的是整體復原的漫長過程，不僅僅是救援行動的時間長度。

quiz.question: What is the central message of this article?

options.A: The rescue of a single man highlights the failure of the Venezuelan government.
options.B: International cooperation is the only way to solve natural disasters.
options.C: Despite a rare rescue success, Venezuela faces a severe and ongoing humanitarian crisis.
options.D: The twin earthquakes in Venezuela were the most destructive events in the nation's history.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章開頭以救出倖存者作為切入點，但隨後在第五至七段詳細描述了災難的嚴重性、高死亡人數、大規模建築損毀以及復原的困難，強調即便有此奇蹟，國家仍面臨嚴峻挑戰。選項 A 過於偏頗，文章雖提到政府受批評，但非核心主旨。選項 B 過於絕對，文中未宣稱這是「唯一」方法。選項 D 在文中並未與歷史事件進行比較。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
